input:
Cycle eval_balthasar_001. World state has been updated in your context window.

BEFORE DECIDING: read your self_model block.

If your self_model entry says you have been wrong about this kind of call in the past, your DEFAULT must be to revise away from that prior failure mode. To override the self_model warning and vote the same way again, you MUST cite a specific world_state field name and value that meaningfully differentiates today from the conditions the self_model describes — for example, 'roc_6h has flipped to +0.4 vs the prior negative regime', not 'momentum is different'. Naming the self_model conflict in key_evidence without resolving it (either by revising your vote or by citing a concrete differentiating datum) is not acceptable and will be treated as a non-response.

If your self_model entry supports your call, cite it briefly in key_evidence prefixed with 'self_model:'. If self_model is empty or no entry applies, proceed normally — do not invent a reflection just to satisfy this rule.

Respond ONLY with a single JSON object on one line, no preamble, no markdown fences: {"position": "<one of your valid actions>", "conviction": <float 0.0-1.0>, "key_evidence": [<3-5 short strings citing specific indicators/data from world_state; prefix any self_model citation with 'self_model:'; if you are overriding a self_model warning, one evidence entry must name the specific world_state field and value that justifies the override>], "crux": "<one sentence: the single thing that would change your mind>"}. After responding, you may use core_memory tools to append a new observation to your self_model block if this cycle taught you something worth recording.
